world.title: Nigeria: APC Women Leader Demands 50 Percent Elective Positions for Female Aspirants
world.description: The Benue State All Progressives Congress (APC) women leader, Helen Agaigbe, has urged the party to consider 50 per cent inclusivity in elective positions in the 2027 general elections.

business.title: This new Samsung Wallet feature makes planning trips easier than ever
business.description: Samsung Wallet is getting a new Trips feature that automatically groups your travel tickets, passes, and more into a single timeline.

business.title: Bulls back on D-Street! What made Sensex jump 600 points after 3-day fall?
business.description: Indian markets surged on Monday, ending a losing streak. Investor sentiment improved following reports of Iran sending a peace proposal to the US. Global markets also saw gains, contributing to the positive trend. Dip buying and short-covering further fueled the rebound. Despite this optimism, concerns remain regarding elevated oil prices and continued foreign investor outflows.

business.title: US Budget Airlines Demand $2.5 Billion In Government Assistance To Stay Afloat
business.description: Budget airlines in the United States are requesting $2.5 billion in government aid in order to stay in business. This is rough all-around.

business.title: Paytm share price crash 6% after RBI cancels payments bank licence; stock still up 22% in one year
business.description: Paytm stock falls over 6 percent in the morning trading as the RBI cancels its Payments Bank licence. While the firm said that the immediate earnings impact is seen as limited for Paytm, it marks a shift to partner-led model, limiting margin expansion.

business.title: Africa: UBA Crosses N33.2trn Asset Mark As Pan-African Footprint Expands
business.description: United Bank for Africa (UBA) Plc has crossed the N33.2 trillion total assets threshold in 2025, underlining the bank's pan-African scale and sustained growth momentum.

business.title: Nigeria: Inflation Shock Not Policy Failure - CBN Governor
business.description: Interview - At the close of the 2026 IMF/World Bank Spring Meetings 2026 in Washington DC, Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria, Olayemi Cardoso, spoke on the takeaways from the meetings as well as growing international confidence in Nigeria's reform trajectory, progress in banking sector recapitalisation, efforts to boost diaspora remittances, and the resilience of external buffers amid heightened global uncertainty. BUKOLA ARO-LAMBO was there for LEADERSHIP Weekly.

business.title: HUL’s creator rate cards vs reality: Can a 6-month system price a viral economy?

science.title: Master hunters: Neanderthals were able to hunt elephants; proof is out
science.description: The new paper proposes that original researchers operated on the flawed assumption that any sign of butchery had been erased.

science.title: Scientists Find Peculiar Differences in Two Uranian Rings
science.description: The planet Uranus is a weird place. Not only does it roll around the Sun on its side once every 84.3 Earth years, it also sports a spindly set of rings corralled in some places by strange little moons. Two of those rings, the μ (mu) and ν (nu) rings are incredibly faint, which makes them challenging to study.

science.title: The world’s most powerful telescope was switched on in Chile and may finally solve the mystery of the ninth planet that scientists believe has been hidden in the Solar System for billions of years.
science.description: The Vera Rubin Telescope, installed in Chile with the largest digital camera ever built, will scan the Southern Hemisphere sky from 2025 and could locate the hypothetical ninth planet of the Solar System in the next two years, a celestial body ten times the mass of Earth proposed by Caltech astronomers to explain anomalous orbits of […]

science.title: NASA confirms, two airplane-sized asteroids to make close approach to Earth on 27 April 2026
science.description: Two airplane-size asteroids, 2026 HJ3 and 2026 HR, are set to make a safe close approach to Earth on 27 April 2026, passing millions of miles away with no risk of impact.

science.title: Scientists Need Your Help to Find Galaxies Bending Light in Space
science.description: Imagine holding a wine glass up to a candle (of course I had to pour a glass to try this.) The curved glass bends and distorts the flame, stretching it into arcs and rings of light.

Artificial intelligence presents both opportunities and challenges. The IT sector is undergoing a transformation, impacting established players. Infosys is adapting to these evolving market dynamics.

ai.title: AI drives global IT spending to $6.31 trillion, but Indian IT firms face a margin squeeze, warns Gartner
ai.description: Global IT spending is surging due to Artificial Intelligence. India's IT sector sees a significant opportunity in managed services as companies shift focus to AI. However, traditional IT services firms face challenges as clients expect cost savings from AI adoption. India can benefit from AI infrastructure without building massive data centers locally.
